draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer approachable, friendly interfaces over corporate/strict ones
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design should feel human and inviting; soften edges throughout (buttons, cards, inputs)
facade.rejectImplies: Design can embrace cleaner, more geometric forms; users may prefer clarity over coziness

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer approachable, friendly interfaces over corporate/strict ones
evidence.implication: Design should feel human and inviting; soften edges throughout (buttons, cards, inputs)

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a CRM that feels like talking to a friend, not reading corporate documentation
facade.label: Casual chat tone
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should be conversational, use contractions, occasional humor, feel approachable and warm
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should be formal, polished, corporate-standard, prioritize authority and polish over warmth

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prioritize visual scanning over dense tables when managing multiple client relationships
facade.axisTargeted: cards vs tables — visual layout density
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: CRM should use modular card-based layouts for client overviews, contact info, and project status
facade.rejectImplies: CRM should use compact tables and spreadsheet-style layouts to maximize data density per screen

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers working late may prefer a cool, low-energy visual environment that reduces eye strain and signals 'focus time'
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: Users value a calm, night-friendly aesthetic; the CRM should feel restful and professional rather than bright and energetic
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer bright, warm, daylight-oriented interfaces; they associate darkness with coldness or difficulty, not productivity

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer animated button states and transitions over instant feedback when confirming actions like marking invoices paid or completing projects
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid transitions vs snappy instant feedback
facade.label: Buttons that slide in
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use smooth, motion-forward interactions for confirmation moments—delightful micro-animations that reward user action and build confidence in high-stakes events
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should prioritize speed and clarity over motion—buttons snap instantly, confirmations appear without delay, every interaction gets out of the way fast

facade.hypothesis: For a freelancer CRM, a persistent left sidebar navigation combined with card-based layouts for clients/projects provides better context awareness and faster task switching than full-page tabs.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure + content organization (sidebar vs tabs, cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values spatial consistency, persistent context, and visual hierarchy. Likely prefers organized workspaces with clear information zones over flat, scrolling interfaces.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer minimal chrome, full-width content views, or tab-based navigation. Possibly values screen real estate and focused single-task flows over always-visible navigation.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers working varied hours need interfaces that reduce eye strain and feel less corporate than bright white dashboards
facade.label: Dark background, light text
facade.acceptImplies: Design should offer dark mode or dark-first aesthetic; prioritize eye comfort for extended work sessions
facade.rejectImplies: Light/bright interface is preferred; dark mode is less critical to the CRM experience for this audience

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool or a supportive coach
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
Whether user prefers numbers/metrics front-and-center vs. narrative summaries and stories—reveals how they track success and make decisions
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals patience level and preference for control vs. automation

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers numbers/metrics front-and-center vs. narrative summaries and stories—reveals how they track success and make decisions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals patience level and preference for control vs. automation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo
